reviews.title: fitbit 3 perforated bands
reviews.text: I purchased these after market bands because the 'large' I got with my fitbit 3 was WAY too big.  It's amazing how much better 1" less in the band fits.  It comes with three colors.  The only thing I am not really super happy about is the silver tone buckle - I am nervous about nickle content as it is an allergy thing for me. The original is black and matches the band. You get 3 full bands, One black... one gray.. and one light aqua blue - it's more of a very light gray blue.  They are a rubbery ? silicone like? material. Smooth, no sharp edges.  They attached easily. So far, so good.  Having said that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Garmin Forerunner® 955 Solar, GPS Running Smartwatch with Solar Charging Capabilities, Tailored to Triathletes, Long-Lasting Battery, Whitestone
description: Lead the pack with Forerunner® 955 Solar, the premium GPS running and triathlon smartwatch with Power Glass™ solar charging to extend battery life. The training readiness feature tells you when you’re primed for a productive session — while daily suggested workouts adapt to your performance and recovery alongside the race widget, which provides training tips, course details and completion time predictions. With morning report, you can get an overview of your sleep, recovery, training and more — as well as a picture of your overall health with heart rate variability (HRV) status (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics). On the run, new multi-band GPS and full-color, built-in maps help you navigate confidently. And with traditional buttons and a touch display, it has never been easier to tap into your training status and know whether you’re training productively, peaking or strained. If you’re pushing toward the podium — or to the very limits — Forerunner 955 Solar is by your side with up to 20 days of battery life with solar charging in smartwatch mode (assuming all-day wear with 3 hours per day outside in 50,000 lux conditions).
features: Built with an always-on, full-color display that’s light on the wrist and easy to read even in direct sunlight

Battery life: up to 20 days of battery life in smartwatch mode for a full picture of your health — from sleep to training (assuming all-day wear with 3 hours per day outside in 50,000 lux conditions); up to 42 hours in GPS mode and up to 49 hours with solar charging (assuming continuous use for entire period in 50,000 lux conditions); up to 110 hours in UltraTrac mode with solar charging (assuming use in 50,000 lux conditions)
Click or swipe through maps and stats with traditional button controls or a new, responsive touchscreen
Whether you’re running on city streets or riding densely covered trails, Forerunner 955 provides full-color, built-in mapping to keep you on track

Access multiple global navigation satellite systems, and get access to multiple frequencies sent by navigation satellites for improved accuracy in areas where GNSS signals are reflected, weak or typically don't penetrate — giving you superior accuracy for how far and how fast you’ve run — even in challenging environments
From the moment you wake up, get a training readiness score based on your sleep quality, recovery, training and more, so you can determine whether it’s a good day to go hard — or take it easy
Get ready for your next race with race widget — which provides training tips, personalized daily suggested workouts and completion time predictions based on course details, weather and performance

Using new indicators such as HRV status, your recent exercise history and performance, get an easy insight into your overall effort with training status — and know whether you’re training productively, peaking or strained

Receive an overview of your sleep, recovery and training outlook as soon as you wake up with morning report — alongside HRV status, training readiness and weather; you can even customize your report to show what you want to see (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics)

With HRV status, gain a deeper understanding of your overall health, recovery and training performance through heart rate variability while you sleep, based on technology developed by our Firstbeat Analytics team (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics)

reviews.title: A great watch for a runner
reviews.text: Haven't found anything I don't like about this watch.  Have begun following it's training advice, and it appears to be working.  Provides a wealth of interesting information.  One real plus for us old guys: the face is larger than the 735XT making it easier for these old eyes to read it.

reviews.title: Buena compra
reviews.text: Llego excelente, bien empacado

reviews.title: Resumen de datos y métricas de seguimiento
reviews.text: Verdaderamente útil, el seguimiento de sueño, entreno, potencia, y muchos mas datos que nos permiten dar un seguimiento más preciso del estado físico.

reviews.title: VO2 and race prediction are garbage
reviews.text:
For some reason with the 955 - Garmin decided to stop updating VO2 and race prediction - so if you have come to rely/look at that - don't get this watch. They have tried fixing it with the last few beta updates, but they can't seem to figure out how to correct it.

Multi-band GPS is nice - which honestly is the only reason we all haven't moved back to the 945/735 - which are AMAZING watches.

reviews.title: Fantastic update
reviews.text:
The 955 is a fantastic update over the previous 9XX, 7XX, and 6XX models. This update I'd much more communicative and provides loads more encouraging updates and workout suggestions. The interface is also more fluid than previous models. The return of touch to the user interface is nice but, optional.

Definitely worth going with

reviews.title: Everything and more from what i expected from a smart watch
reviews.text: i spent a great deal of time researching smart watches, Garmin came up the winner.  I have dealt with their customer service on two occasions with another product and found them to be excellent which i a big reason for buying another Garmin product.

reviews.title: Poor battery life, feature rich
reviews.text:
If only the battery life was better... I have the GPS turned off and I only measure my blood oxygen level when I'm asleep yet it is nearly impossible for the watch to last a little more than 24 hours.

My next Garmin will be the one with a solar powered assist.

That being said for the negatives this watch has the features I wanted at a good price:

- Sleep quality/quantity
- Blood oxygen level
- Steps counter
- Flight of stairs counting
- Water intake recording ) manual input)
- Multiple activities recording (it can detect when I'm running, bike riding, etc.)

It has been durable and the screen is still scratch free.

If poor battery life isn't a deal breaker buy it.

reviews.title: After a year and a half...can't wait to switch, Spotify is the worst part of this watch by far
reviews.text:
I have owned this watch for a year and a half now. I use it almost daily for my walks/runs, weight workouts, and indoor bike workouts. The main reason I chose this watch in the first place was because it was one of the smaller ones available. I am 5'9 but have small wrists, and really disliked the 'utilitarian' look of larger watches. After a year and a half, I am so tired of the issues with it and ready to jump ship. If I used this watch more regularly (I only wear it for workouts - not all day), I would have switched a LONG time ago. The amount of people who have complained about the same issues for YEARS without Garmin doing anything makes me hesitant to purchase another Garmin product, especially with the Apple Watch, Samsung, and now Google buying out Fitbit and coming up with a watch.
- Spotify on this watch seriously sucks. If you check Spotify forums, you will find COUNTLESS threads of suggestions for the Spotify app on this watch that should truly be basic features at this price. Do they implement them or listen? No. Also, there is no way to contact Spotify customer service - you just get pointed towards their 'community forum' where you have people complaining of the same exact things with no fix. You can't skip forward on any track, at all. I listen to a lot of podcasts when I walk. Some are 2 hours in length while my walk is only 1 hour. This watch constantly either reverts to the very beginning of my podcast so I have to start all over (because again, you can't skip forward - so have fun listening to ads as well!) in anything, only updates 1 song out of 15 on my playlist or get the error 'sync failed', or some other random issue. Also, if you have a playlist on Spotify, you cannot choose the track you want to listen to. You have to hit the >> button to get to where you want. Really? I hope Garmin drops Spotify because they have been a truly horrible app on this watch the entire time with no effort to make updates. Take a look at the reviews of Spotify on the Garmin store if you don't believe me.
- Battery life is a couple of days. However, there are points in time where my watch will be at say 80% the night before, I get ready to leave and go my walk the next morning, pick up my watch, and it's at 8%. WTF? This happens enough to be super annoying and apparently is not an 'only me' problem. It appears to be tied to when the watch updates.
- I tried to wear this to track my sleep in the beginning and gave up after two nights due to the extreme disco effect of the 'gesture' motions. I tried turning on the 'do not disturb' function advertised on the watch which is supposed to disable this stupidly bright light that WILL wake you and whoever is next to you up. Didn't work. Do a search for 'Garmin watch lights up in middle of the night' and you will find all sorts of people complaining of the same thing, and not just on this version of the watch.
- GPS has issues connecting and can take several minutes which is pretty annoying when you try to start tracking an activity only to see a red bar when trying to connect. I 'sync' my watch to my phone constantly so that is not the issue. I have switched between the GPS options Garmin offers on the watch to no avail. Search for this issue as well and you will see lots of people with the same complaint.

reviews.title: GET THIS WATCH!!!
reviews.text: I LOVE this watch!!! It is so easy to use, tracks all of the metrics I am interested in (steps, sleep, stress, activities, hydration, menstrual cycle) and then some!! It is very accurate and the app is very user- friendly. The watch itself has worked perfectly and I have found it to be comfortable and intuitive to use.  It holds a charge for several days on end and when it does need to be charged, it reaches full battery very quickly! I looked at a lot of different watches and considered a lot of different options. I am so happy that I went with the vivoactive 4! Garmin, you all did a great job with this watch!! I honestly could not be happier with my choice!!

reviews.title: A Gift that went well
reviews.text:
This was a Christmas gift for my wife to replace her older Vivosmart. She wanted the new features but she has a small wrist.
Doing some searches I thought this would be as big as I should try.

All went well. It does all she needs and while it's much better it's not too big. Doing larger does add more battery time and more features, but this is the right fit for her

title: SAMSUNG GEAR S3 FRONTIER Smartwatch 46MM (Bluetooth Only) - Dark Grey (Renewed)
description:
Stay in touch, without reaching for your phone, with the smartwatch that’s engineered for adventure. Convenience being the key feature, the watch offers fitness tracking, app access and watch face customization. With the Gear S3, you can pay right from your wrist and track your location as well. The Gear S3 is compatible with both Android and iOS smartphones.
Never Miss a Moment
The Gear S3 is Bluetooth connected so you can receive texts, emails, and alerts without reaching for your phone. With a built-in speaker and microphone, you can also make and answer calls hands-free. Just rotate the distinctive steel bezel to easily access apps and notifications so you can instantly check texts, updates, and more.
Pay with Your Watch
With Samsung Pay on your Gear S3, you can easily make payments with your watch almost anywhere you can swipe or tap a credit card - even without your phone or wallet.
Get Out There
Whether you’re off-roading or exploring a new city, the built-in GPS tracks your activity and makes it easy to share your location.
Designed for The Elements
The military-grade Gear S3 is tough enough to handle the elements. It resists water, dust, extreme temperatures, and the occasional drop so you can venture out with confidence.
Go for Days
Go nonstop for days on a single charge.4 When it’s time to recharge, the wireless charger lets you power up without slowing down.
Tailored to You
Make the Gear S3 your own. Customize your watch face, choosing from thousands of striking colors and designs. Plus easily swap out the standard 22mm band.
features: The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
Text, call and get notifications directly from your watch through your Bluetooth connected smartwatch.
Access the apps you use every day on your Gear S3. Catch a ride, check scores, stream music, and more.
With the built-in S Health app, track your steps, monitor your heart rate, and more.

reviews.title: Just over 90 days and it completely died
reviews.text: Rrally liked this watch at first. The battery did not last very long, even after adjusting settings. Thought it was the battery so we bought a new one, nope just completely died now. The new battery is fine, charges up and works, but the watch will not even turn on with new battery in it.

reviews.title: Love this
reviews.text: Love this watch

reviews.title: Husband LOVES This
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband for his birthday and Father's Day and he loves it. He used to have a Galaxy S9, but dropped it into a lake while we were fishing *face palm*. But he ended up getting an old S7 from a family member and the watch connects with that as well. The only complaint I have heard from him is about the band, which was easy and cheap to replace with a metal material that didn't stick to his wrist as much as the rubbery band that comes with the watch.

reviews.title: Great looking watch!
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband and he loves it!  He answers his phone and text messages right on the watch.  He doesn't have an excuse not to answer me now :D

reviews.title: Good watch
reviews.text:
I bought this when it first came out, I needed it's walking and stair climbing metrics.

It's big, heavy, and if you're at all active, it's on the charge cradle for around 1 hour. The big, heavy part, some like it, I wish it were a little smaller. The battery life, it's an early version, I didn't expect much, my next watch, 20 day minimum.

All in all, it's a very functional watch, feature laden, and good, as long as Samsung supports the OS

reviews.title: Good watch
reviews.text: Battery life u is good ,

reviews.title: Amazing Feature Packed Smart Watch at a Spectacular Cost
reviews.text:
This VALYV K22 Military Sport Watch is packed with an amazing number of smart features and provided seamless integration with my Samsung smart phone. The display is crystal-clear, and the audio capability and volume are very impressive. In general, it’s a very handsome watch and has a very intuitive user interface. The price is unbelievably low in comparison to some of the other smart watches on the market.
I especially enjoy the built-in fitness, activity, and sports tracking features which have already proven to be invaluable while training at the gym as well as general health monitoring during my activity throughout the day.
This watch charges quickly using the included USB magnetically connected cable and the battery is long lasting once charged. The user interface is customizable through the app – I preferred the default one that it shipped with… but there are MANY other options to choose from.
I summary, after spending many years not even wearing a watch… I am very happy with this purchase and can definitely recommend the purchase of this useful product to others.

reviews.title: Really great for the price!
reviews.text: I don’t spend a lot of money on things like this so I didn’t know what to expect since it’s not $300+… it’s not bad for the price! It works well, the stats seem fairly accurate, except the blood pressure which is always showing ridiculously good (I wish)!! I have high blood pressure and this watch has me at 117/ 68 most of the time. Other then that the only problem I have is the charge! It has a cable with 2 tiny magnets, you have to line them up just so and as soon as you’ve got it, it pops off!!

reviews.title: Finally a comfortable stretchy band
reviews.text: This watch band, that I have for my FitBit Inspire 2 is just what I was looking for. I hated the band that came with it.  I tried another buckle, cloth band, which was ok, but still a challenge to take on and off of my wrist.  This band is easy to attach to the Fit bit watch, and the length is adjustable (which I think is unusual for a slip-on stretchy band.  I even am ok with the multi color.  It makes it easy to pair up with lots of tops.  It can be adjusted in length to most everyone's wrist.  I have a very small wrist (short woman here) and I was able to make it small enough for me. reviews.title: Not a Good Choice for Me
reviews.text:
Like - was able to call and receive texts. Was able to answer a call on the watch. Pairing was easy. The app was easy.
Dislike - Once paired, the watch takes all your media volume away from your phone. You won't hear any music like from YouTube, text message alert or phone ringing. The watch has a little vibration that you can barely feel, when you receive a text or getting a phone call. All sounds like from Facebook videos or anything, goes through the watch. The only way I could find to get it to quit doing this, was to unpair the watch totally. I need military time but couldn't find a way to make the time in a 24 hour format. You have to put the watch together. The bands come apart from the watch. Everytime you move your hand the watch face lights up. This was definitely not what I was looking for at all. Would I buy this again? No.

reviews.title: Seems nice

Seems like a nice watch. It has a standard user interface for a smart watch.

I like the design and think that it seems nice

reviews.title: Works well for step counting and can measure heartrate upon request. Otherwise less than stellar
reviews.text:
This generally works well enough for exercise purposes. I didn't get the "dangerous app warning" on my iPhone, though I did get a warning that if I use the watch to display messages, then the app has access to all my contacts and the text of all my messages. I doubt I'm a target for Chinese spying so I chose to ignore that, but it's still worth noting.

I love that it's waterproof, other watch faces are available (though I couldn't figure out how to restore the original, more-helpful one without doing a full factory reset), it tracks steps and sleep, plus it's able to measure heartrate and blood pressure. Mine sometimes tells me the weather, but only immediately after connecting to my Google account on my phone; otherwise it just says "unknown" (and honestly, if I have to click extra buttons on my phone, I can just check the weather there). It has a music playback control, but I couldn't get that to work. It also theoretically connects to my phone and lets me enter a phone number, but I couldn't click "dial" so that bit felt more like a child's toy, where you pretend to call a phone but don't actually do it. But since I use this for exercise more than connectivity, I'm OK with that.

Some things are less than stellar, like their heart rate screen always shows a default chart (I don't think it ever lets you create your own heart rate chart)... and it shows a heartrate from 120-150 ALWAYS, which catches me off guard when I've been sitting at my desk for 2 hours. But I learned it's just a concept; there is no real *graph of my heartrate that I can find, just current / min / max that I can see from my phone. Or I can click "measure" to get my instant heartrate on my phone (good while exercising). Blood pressure can sometimes be measured from your phone (though it takes 1-4 minutes for some reason), and sometimes that screen disappears completely; I haven't yet figured out the pattern

It doesn't seem to measure many things other than steps automatically; you have to select each one when you're wanting to see it. Soooooooo all told it's solidly mediocre. It's good for counting steps, manually checking heartrate, and manually checking blood pressure (very slowly) on occasion; otherwise it's a lot weaker than most smart phones

The watch has a waterproof rating of IP68, which makes it shower proof and wearable in a swimming pool. It would be safe even to submerge up to a depth of 1.5 metres for around half an hour. The strap is changeable and made of soft silicone, very comfortable.

【More Functions & Quality Services】There are other convenient functions, such as music control/drink water reminder/timer/weather reminder/blood oxygen monitor/stopwatch/alarm/breathing training. The functions are easy to access and use. The app syncs well and quickly with the watch. If you encounter any quality or use problems, please feel free to contact us through order and we will give you a satisfactory answer.

reviews.title: Seems to work pretty well for the most part
reviews.text: Setup was easy with a quick app download and it paired with my iPhone 12 without any drama. Biometric tracking seems to work reasonably well with the exception of heart rate, which seems to always read 70 no matter what I'm doing. I'm not too crazy about the aesthetic of the watch, it just looks kind of cheap to me. I got this mainly to use as a health tracking tool and to tell time, not to make and receive calls/texts with, so I haven't setup any of those features yet and may never get around to doing so. So i can't comment on ease of use for any of those features. I did like that there are quite a lot of different watch face designs that can be applied and without any additional purchase. For the price of around $40, this is a pretty smokin' deal for a smart watch, I think.

reviews.title: Not bad for the price
reviews.text:
I’ve tried a few different affordable watches and my biggest issue has been the charging port. They have trouble staying connected with tiny pins and no magnetic pull. So going into this I’m more critical.
Upside is there is definitely a better charging system. It holds in place nicely even though it’s a pretty small pin area it is easier to charge.
The screen does have low sensitivity response. When pairing it it took 3 tries to get it recognize I had tapped the screen.
On a good note it’s reading well and has some great features like heart rate, oxygen, and sleep. Everything lined up well with my normal statistics so I feel it’s pretty accurate.
There’s a variety of images to choose from so you can customize the screen however you would like.
It’s very comfortable. The band holds like a premium watch band. It has a large face that is classy. Aesthetically it’s very nice.
Overall for an affordable smart watch it’s a decent option.

reviews.title: Okay tracker
reviews.text: For the price, it is an okay tracker.  The microphone on the watch is not of great quality as I was told sounded muffled on it.  It does have a lot of features and was quite easy to use but nothing about it made it stand out from the other hundreds of smart watches out on the market.

reviews.title: Cute watch is all I can say
reviews.text: Initially the watch was quick to charge and holds the charge for a couple of days.  The functions were easy to use, and a lot to choose from for the activities I do.  I mainly track my steps, distance, duration, heart rate.  It worked well for a few weeks, then I took it swimming for a few laps in the pool.  That was a mistake, battery stopped working (won't charge anymore).  Sad, I really liked the watch, it's cute but the battery died.

reviews.title: Great Smart watch for the essentials
reviews.text:
I was looking for a good smart watch for my mother. She was a Fitbit user but her Fitbit died after years of use and wanted something with a similar feel.
This watch really worked out for her.
It's got a great screen and a good amount of apps to provide essential uses.
It's able to work with iOS and Android.
It's able to get notifications from apps and make phone calls.
Not a bad product at all.

reviews.title: It's ok. But you can get a Fitbit style watch for less
reviews.text: I wear it near my pulse because I feel it gives you a more accurate reading. The sleep monitor sucks. It didn't start monitoring my sleep until I turned it around and put the sensors on my inner wrist

reviews.title: Sent back twice because the blood pressure measurement was inaccurate
reviews.text: I had some confidence in buying this at first.  It connected to app nicely and did well with sleep measurement when I tried that.  I also liked the face choices.  However, having a reasonably accurate blood pressure measurement as long as it is included as a feature was very important for me.  It always read about the same measurement of 140/86, but that was not accurate when compared to a cuff style.  It also read the same for my wife who is always different.  I returned one, got an easy replacement and had the same issue.  Returned for refund this time.  I did not check if waterproof, but that was also an important feature.

reviews.title: just what I was hoping for
reviews.text: It did not come with extra screws, which was a bummer.  However I didn't end up needing them, so it worked out.  The new band was incredibly tricky to get on the fit bit itself, I had to enlist my husband's help.  Between the two of us, we managed to get it on.  It didn't help that the holes didn't perfectly line up and were not drilled out completely on the band, luckily the material is stretchy enough that we could work with it/around it.  I would buy it again if I had to.
